parts.description:
An action button inside a toast (e.g. "Undo"). Unlike {@link RdxToastClose} it does not dismiss
on its own — wire your handler with `(click)` and call `close()` from the exposed context when
the action should also close the toast.
Button that dismisses its toast. Put it on a native `<button>` for built-in semantics.

Wraps a toast's inner parts (title, description, actions) — the Angular counterpart of
`<Toast.Content>`. Headless: it carries no styles; consumers apply `overflow: hidden` to clip
taller toasts during the stack/expand animation. It mirrors the root's `data-state` so content
can transition together with the root.
Supporting body text for a toast; registers so `aria-describedby` only targets a real node.

Moves the toast viewport to a different part of the DOM — the Angular counterpart of
`<Toast.Portal>`. Defaults to `document.body`; pass `container` to target another element.

Positions an anchored toast against an element — the Angular counterpart of `<Toast.Positioner>`.

A "thin" positioner (ADR 0012): it inherits the popper positioning surface (inputs, `placed`
output, unified vars + placement attrs) from {@link RdxPopperContentWrapper}. Bind `anchor` (and
optionally `side`/`align`/offsets) — typically from a toast's `positionerProps`. It also composes
its own {@link RdxPopper} so it is self-contained (no surrounding `rdxPopperRoot` needed), and
mirrors the anchor width / transform origin as friendlier `--toast-*` CSS variables.

inputs.name: align
inputs.description: How to align the popup relative to the specified side. May change when collisions occur.

inputs.name: alignOffset
inputs.description:
An offset in pixels from the `start` or `end` alignment options. Also accepts an
{@link OffsetFunction} (same signature as `sideOffset`).

inputs.name: anchor
inputs.description: An element to position the popup against. Defaults to the popper anchor.

inputs.name: arrowPadding
inputs.description:
Minimum distance to maintain between the arrow and the edges of the popup.
If your content has border-radius, this will prevent it from overflowing the corners.

inputs.name: avoidCollisions
inputs.description:
When `true`, overrides the `side` and `align` preferences to prevent collisions with boundary edges.

`false` disables all avoidance (unless a `collisionAvoidance` object is provided, which wins).

inputs.name: collisionAvoidance
inputs.description:
How the popup avoids colliding with the boundary edges, per axis (Base UI `collisionAvoidance`).
Overrides the deprecated `avoidCollisions` and any per-primitive preset. An object here **fully
replaces** the preset — omitted fields fall back to `side: 'flip'`, `align: 'flip'`,
`fallbackAxisSide: 'end'`, never to the preset. See {@link RdxCollisionAvoidance}.

inputs.name: collisionBoundary
inputs.description:
The element used as the collision boundary.
By default this is the viewport, though you can provide additional element(s) to be included in this check.

inputs.name: collisionPadding
inputs.description:
The distance in pixels from the boundary edges where collision detection should occur.
Accepts a number (same for all sides), or a partial padding object, for example: `{ top: 20, left: 20 }`.

inputs.name: dir
inputs.description:
Text direction, used only to resolve a logical `side` (`inline-start` / `inline-end`) to a
physical one. Falls back to an enclosing direction provider, then `ltr`.

inputs.name: hideWhenDetached
inputs.description: Whether to hide the content when the trigger becomes fully occluded.

inputs.name: positionStrategy
inputs.description: The type of CSS position property to use.

inputs.name: side
inputs.description:
The preferred side of the anchor to render against when open.
Will be reversed when collisions occur and avoidCollisions is enabled.

Accepts the logical `'inline-start'` / `'inline-end'` in addition to the physical sides; those
resolve to `left` / `right` for the text direction (Base UI parity). The placed side reported via
`data-side` / `placedSide()` echoes the requested kind: logical in → logical out (even after a
collision flip), physical in → physical out.

inputs.name: sideOffset
inputs.description:
Distance between the anchor and the popup in pixels. Also accepts an {@link OffsetFunction} that
reads the anchor / positioner dimensions and the resolved side / align.

inputs.name: sticky
inputs.description:
The sticky behavior on the align axis. `partial` will keep the
content in the boundary as long as the trigger is at least partially
in the boundary whilst "always" will keep the content in the boundary
regardless.

inputs.name: updatePositionStrategy
inputs.description: Strategy to update the position of the floating element on every animation frame.

outputs.name: placed
outputs.description: Emits when the element is placed.

parts.description:
Hosts a {@link RdxToastManager} for its subtree. Put it on a wrapping element (or the app root)
so descendant viewports and components share one queue. `limit` caps simultaneously visible toasts.

inputs.name: limit
inputs.description: Max number of toasts shown at once.

inputs.name: timeout
inputs.description: Default auto-dismiss delay (ms) for toasts that omit their own `timeout`. `0` disables it.

parts.description:
A single toast — the Angular counterpart of `<Toast.Root>`. Bind the toast model from the
viewport's `@for`; this directive owns the announcement `role`, the `data-state` enter/leave
contract, swipe-to-dismiss, and the stacking variables consumers style against.

Stacking / styling contract written to the host (no transform is applied for you):
- `--toast-index` — position from the front (`0` = frontmost).
- `--toast-height` — this toast's measured height (px).
- `--toast-offset-y` — combined height (px) of the toasts stacked in front, for expanded layout.
- `--toast-swipe-movement-x` / `--toast-swipe-movement-y` — live swipe offset (px).
- `[data-front]` — present on the frontmost toast.
- `[data-expanded]` — present while the viewport is hovered/focused.
- `[data-state]` — `open` while visible, `closed` once dismissal begins.
- `[data-swiping]` / `[data-swipe-direction]` / `[data-swipe-dismiss]` — gesture state.

When the leave animation (driven by `data-state="closed"`) ends, the toast leaves the queue.

inputs.name: swipeDirection
inputs.description: Allowed swipe-to-dismiss directions. Accepts a single direction or a list.

inputs.name: toast
inputs.description: The toast model to render — pass the item from the viewport's `@for`.

parts.description: Accessible title for a toast; registers so the root only points `aria-labelledby` at a real node.

The positioned region that holds the visible toasts — the Angular counterpart of
`<Toast.Viewport>`. Exposes the queue for templates to render, and while it is hovered or focused
it expands the stack (`data-expanded` on each root) and pauses every auto-dismiss timer, resuming
once neither hover nor focus remains — matching Base UI.

Headless: it carries no positioning styles. Consumers position it (e.g. fixed bottom-right) and
iterate `viewport.toasts()` with `@for`.
